name: AB Pawn & Gun
description_short: AB Pawn & Gun Inc is a licensed pawnbroker specializing in firearms and other collateral-based loans in West Park, FL. Note: Their FFL expired May 1, 2023.
description_long:
AB Pawn & Gun Inc operates as a federally licensed pawnbroker in West Park, Florida, holding Federal Firearms License number 1-59-011-02-3E-35466 under the classification of 'Pawnbroker in Firearms Other Than Destructive Devices.' The business is located at 2103 S State Road 7 and is regulated by the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF). The company has been in the pawn and firearms business, serving the South Florida community.

As a licensed pawnbroker, AB Pawn & Gun Inc offers collateral-based loans where customers can pledge firearms, jewelry, and other valuables in exchange for immediate cash. Their licensing under ATF regulations indicates they handle firearm transactions and pawn services. The business provides an alternative to traditional lending for consumers who need quick cash and have personal property to use as collateral.

A key distinguishing factor is their federal firearms licensing and compliance with ATF regulations, which requires them to maintain strict record-keeping and background check procedures for firearm transactions. This level of federal oversight sets licensed firearms pawnbrokers apart from general pawn shops and signals regulatory compliance in a highly regulated industry.

However, a critical caveat is that AB Pawn & Gun Inc's Federal Firearms License expired on May 1, 2023. The FFLs.com listing specifically notes this is an expired license and directs users to an updated version. Consumers should verify current licensing status and confirm the business is still operating before conducting any transactions. The expired status raises questions about the company's current operational status and legal ability to conduct firearm transactions.

diagnosis: AB Pawn & Gun Inc is potentially relevant for South Florida consumers seeking collateral-based loans against firearms or valuables, but the expired Federal Firearms License (as of May 1, 2023) is a major red flag. Consumers must verify the business's current operational and legal status before approaching them, as the expired license suggests they may no longer be authorized to conduct firearm transactions or may have ceased operations entirely.
